Audit Target: mv_biomass_gasification

  • Executive Summary
  • Evidently Profile
  • Flagged Observations
  • Custom Visuals

The data quality audit for the mv_biomass_gasification dataset reveals a moderate level of concern regarding data integrity and metadata completeness. The most significant finding is the presence of 44 duplicated rows, representing approximately 7.8% of the current dataset. Additionally, a notable discrepancy exists between the current row count (565) and the reference record count (521), suggesting a recent batch upload that may have introduced redundant or un-normalized entries. Statistical anomalies are primarily concentrated in gas composition outputs for ‘Spent Oak Chips’ and ‘Walnut Shells’, specifically involving light hydrocarbons (C2-C6) and primary syngas components (CO, H2).

A critical metadata gap was identified, with 42 records missing unit definitions and 43 records missing provider codenames. Furthermore, all records in the flagged sample lack sample dates and analyst attributions, which hinders temporal trend analysis and accountability. The anomalies in gas concentrations (particularly those with Z-scores > 1.0) appear clustered by provider (‘rigging’ for oak chips and ‘energetic’ for walnut shells), suggesting either specific gasification performance characteristics or localized sensor calibration variances at those facilities. Priority should be given to deduplication and the population of missing unit/provider fields to ensure the dataset’s scientific validity.

Record ID Resource Provider Sample Date Parameter Value Z-Score Severity
